note_text:
Patient: 70F presenting with relapsing relapsing remitting.
Conservative management including bracing, beta-blockers was attempted without adequate improvement.
Imaging studies confirm findings consistent with relapsing remitting.
Referring neurology physician requests authorization for disease-modifying therapy.

Patient: 70F presenting with active relapsing remitting.
No conservative therapy has been documented yet.
Imaging studies confirm findings consistent with relapsing remitting.
Referring neurology physician requests authorization for disease-modifying therapy.

Patient: 76M presenting with relapsing relapsing remitting.
Conservative management including dietary and lifestyle modification, physical therapy was attempted over approximately 16 weeks without adequate improvement.
Imaging studies confirm findings consistent with relapsing remitting.
Referring neurology physician requests authorization for disease-modifying therapy.

Patient: 36F presenting with seizure disorder.
Conservative management including activity modification, antiarrhythmic medication was attempted over approximately 9 weeks without adequate improvement.
Imaging studies confirm findings consistent with seizure disorder.
Referring neurology physician requests authorization for epilepsy surgery.

Patient: 38F presenting with seizure disorder.
No conservative therapy has been documented yet.
Imaging studies confirm findings consistent with seizure disorder.
Referring neurology physician requests authorization for epilepsy surgery.

Patient: 67M presenting with seizure disorder.
Conservative management including physical therapy, corticosteroid injection was attempted over approximately 8 weeks without adequate improvement.
Imaging workup is still pending.
Referring neurology physician requests authorization for epilepsy surgery.

Patient: 75M presenting with frequent headache disorder.
Conservative management including dietary and lifestyle modification, activity modification was attempted over approximately 11 weeks without adequate improvement.
Imaging workup is still pending.
Referring neurology physician requests authorization for cgrp inhibitor.

Patient: 71F presenting with disabling headache disorder.
Conservative management including bracing was attempted over approximately 1 weeks without adequate improvement.
Imaging studies confirm findings consistent with headache disorder.
Referring neurology physician requests authorization for cgrp inhibitor.

Patient: 68F presenting with frequent headache disorder.
No conservative therapy has been documented yet.
Imaging workup is still pending.
Referring neurology physician requests authorization for cgrp inhibitor.

Patient: 38F presenting with refractory parkinson's disease.
Conservative management including beta-blockers, preventive medication trial was attempted over approximately 30 weeks without adequate improvement.
Imaging studies confirm findings consistent with parkinson's disease.
Referring neurology physician requests authorization for deep brain stimulation.

Patient: 32M presenting with refractory parkinson's disease.
Conservative management including antiarrhythmic medication was attempted over approximately 19 weeks without adequate improvement.
Imaging studies confirm findings consistent with parkinson's disease.
Referring neurology physician requests authorization for deep brain stimulation.

Patient: 36M presenting with dyskinesia parkinson's disease.
No conservative therapy has been documented yet.
Imaging workup is still pending.
Referring neurology physician requests authorization for deep brain stimulation.
